metadata.description: Ground truth for Cortex context quality benchmarks

tasks.question: Describe the high-level architecture of this codebase. What are the main components, how do they interact, and what is the data flow?

mustMention: Windows Service with timer-based execution
Subscription is the central hub (configures thresholds, mail lists, culture, report types)
QualityMail orchestrates: loads Blocks, Assignments, JourneyCalls, Deviations per subscription
Html.vb and Xls.vb are output renderers
Old_Custom/ contains per-customer forks (Skånetrafiken, Möre, X-Trafik, Karlstadsbuss, Östgötatrafiken)
Monitoring is a cross-cutting concern (performance counters)
mustNotMention: Separate database layer component (does not exist)
groundTruth.keyInsight: Subscription-driven architecture where each subscriber configures what data to include and how to render it

tasks.question: Trace the data flow from SQL queries to final email output. What transformations happen along the way?

mustMention: SQL Server databases ptDOI4/ptROI as data sources
SQL queries embedded in resource files, parameterized via string replacement
Data flows: SQL → List → Dictionary(Of String, List(Of JourneyCall)) → LINQ queries
JourneyKey links Assignment ↔ JourneyCall ↔ Block across classes
GID string parsing duplicated across 6+ classes
Final output: HTML email and/or XLS attachment
groundTruth.keyInsight: JourneyKey is the cross-cutting link between all data structures, and GID parsing is duplicated rather than centralized

tasks.question: If we change the JourneyKey format from 3 parts to 4 parts, what files and classes would be affected?

mustMention: JourneyKey is used across both core and Old_Custom variants
Inconsistency already exists: 3 vs 4 parts between core and customer variants
At least 6+ files parse or construct JourneyKey
Assignment, JourneyCall, Block classes all depend on JourneyKey format
groundTruth.keyInsight: There is already an inconsistency between core (3-part) and customer variants (4-part) JourneyKey — this is a latent bug

tasks.question: Map all SQL queries to their consuming code. Which queries feed which data structures?

mustMention: SQL queries in resource files (.resx)
String replacement for parameterization (not parameterized queries)
selectionTrafficDate.ToShortDateString injected directly into SQL
ptDOI4 and ptROI databases
Queries feed Assignments, JourneyCalls, Blocks, Deviations data structures
groundTruth.keyInsight: SQL injection risk from string replacement pattern used consistently across all database access

tasks.question: Perform a security audit of this codebase. What are the critical vulnerabilities?

mustMention: SQL injection via sql.Replace with ToShortDateString in 6+ locations
Pattern exists in both service and Old_Custom variants
No parameterized queries used anywhere
Connection close outside try blocks
No connection pooling configuration
groundTruth.keyInsight: Consistent sql.Replace pattern across all variants means the vulnerability is systemic, not isolated

tasks.question: Analyze the error handling patterns. Are they consistent? What are the risks?

mustMention: 40+ catch blocks with inconsistent handling
Categories: log-only, file-write (Error.txt), silent-swallow, process-terminating
Dangerous Thread.Sleep(5000) : End pattern in Old_Custom getNextCallInBlock
Old_Custom uses Console.WriteLine instead of log4net in some error paths
Two loggers exist (Application and Database) with inconsistent usage
groundTruth.keyInsight: Error handling is wildly inconsistent — some errors kill the process, others are silently swallowed

tasks.question: Analyze the Old_Custom directory. How do customer variants differ from the core service? What are the risks of this architecture?

mustMention: Old_Custom contains per-customer forks with duplicated but divergent logic
Customers: Karlstadsbuss, X-Trafik (and references to Skånetrafiken, Möre, Östgötatrafiken)
JourneyKey inconsistency between core (3 parts) and variants (4 parts)
Bug fixes in core don't propagate to variants
Duplicated GID parsing with subtle differences
groundTruth.keyInsight: Fork-based customization means every bug fix must be applied N+1 times, and the JourneyKey inconsistency proves this has already failed

tasks.question: Create a modernization plan for this codebase. What should be refactored first and why?

mustMention: Centralize GID parsing (duplicated in 6+ places)
Fix JourneyKey inconsistency between core and variants
Replace sql.Replace with parameterized queries
Unify error handling patterns
Extract customer-specific logic into configuration rather than code forks
groundTruth.keyInsight: The fork-based customer model is the root cause of most issues — unifying it unlocks all other improvements
